title: jasminum pseudopinnatum
intro: Jasminum pseudopinnatum, also known as the 'False Ashoka' or 'False Jasmine', is a plant species within the family Oleaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Jasminum pseudopinnatum include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, headache, and skin conditions. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various ailments.
